name: Source Fidelity review for Partial Reprogramming bundle
summary: Source-fidelity review accepts revision 1. PubMed ESummary/EFetch and ClinicalTrials.gov API records match the staged source metadata, study classifications, registry status, and bounded finding statements. The staged claims preserve the important source limits: Ocampo and later OSK/OSKM papers remain animal or human-cell evidence, Macip's 109% remaining-lifespan claim is attributed to aged male mice, Abad is treated as a full-reprogramming safety boundary, and ER-100 is represented as a recruiting no-results phase 1 registry rather than efficacy evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: Primary-source checks did not find a blocking mismatch between the staged records and the PubMed or ClinicalTrials.gov records.
findings.why_it_matters: The bundle creates the first auditable source map for this track, so source metadata, endpoint boundaries, registry status, and quantitative notes have to match the cited records before publication.
findings.recommended_action: Accept the source layer for publication; future full-text review can refine effect sizes, sex-specific details, and delivery schedules without blocking this conservative support map.
